titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Adams Papers
titlestmt.author: Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 1, 2021 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English. Some materials in German.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 20 (digital media) and Boxes 21-22 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 16-19 (alphabetical files): Restricted until 5 years after the deaths of Robert Adams and Kerstin Adams. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Photographs, circa 1880-2013. II. Exhibitions, circa 1971-2017. III. Reviews and Press, circa 1970-2018. IV. Early Writings, 1959-1972. V. Artwork, circa 1960-2006. VI. Restricted Papers. VII. Computer and Audiovisual Media.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Adams (1937- ) is an American photographer whose work focuses on the changing landscape of the American West. Adams was born in New Jersey to Ross Adams and Lois Hickman Adams. He received his B.A. in English from Redlands University in 1959 and PhD from University of Southern California in 1965. Adams married Kerstin Mornestam in 1960. He began taking photographs while teaching at Colorado College in 1963, and became a full-time photographer in 1970. Adams has published over 40 books and his photography has been exhibited at galleries and museums throughout the United States and abroad.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Adams Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: 5 boxes of publications were transferred to Yale University Art Gallery in 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the life and career of American photographer Robert Adams, including photographs, correspondence, clippings, writings, posters, printed material, born digital material, and audiovisual material. Photographs include landscape photographs throughout the western United States, portraits, family photographs, and photographs of Adams's exhibitions at galleries and museums throughout the United States and abroad. Also present are posters for Adams's exhibitions, reviews of his books and exhibitions, some of his early writings, and his art, primarily drawings.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robert Adams Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Circa 228 photographs documenting the life, career, and family of Robert Adams. Includes photographs by Robert Adams, his wife Kerstin Adams, and family photographs. Also includes 20 photographs by Robert Adams's grandfather C. A. Hickman, who was also a photographer.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Robert Adams and Adams Family Photographs and Photographs by Others.

did.unittitle: Robert Adams and Adams Family Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Arizona, Colorado, Michigan, Montana, New Mexico, Oregon, Sweden, and Germany, including some landscape photographs by Robert Adams. The subseries also contains family photographs and Adams's portraits of friends and family. Includes photographs of Adams's homes in Michigan, Oregon, and Colorado. Finally, includes Kerstin Adams's photographs of Robert Adams, including portraits, photographs of his studio and work spaces, and several photographs of him taking and developing photographs.

did.unittitle: Color slides of Colorado, Michigan, and Montana
Kerstin Adams portraits of Robert Adams
Photographs of Colorado and New Mexico
Color Slides of Sweden and Germany
Photographs of Coloroado, Oregon, and Germany
Photographs of Robert Adams and Nick Nixon
Kerstin Adams portraits of Robert Adams
Photographs of Robert Adams with proofs for Listening to the River
Kerstin Adams portraits of Robert Adams
Photographs of Kerstin Adams and Judith Ross
Photograph of Robert Adams and Carolyn Dunn
Photographs of Robert Adams and Kerstin Adams in Oregon
Photograph of Rocky Mountain National Park
Photographs of Yampa River Canyon and Split Mountain Canyon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by C. A. Hickman and other photographers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: C. A. Hickman Photographs and Other Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 negatives and 16 prints by Robert Adams's grandfather C. A. Hickman. Photographs depict landscapes and people in Nebraska, Iowa, and South Dakota.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Some of the prints are Hickman's originals while others are negatives made by Richard Benson, or copy prints made by Adams from Benson's negatives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into two subseries: Photographs of Exhibitions and Exhibition Posters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs and posters of Robert Adams's exhibitions in the United States and abroad.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Robert Adams's photography exhibitions at galleries and museums in the United States and abroad. Some photographs depict the installation and preparation for exhibitions, while others show visitors viewing the exhibitions. In several cases, there are photographs of the same exhibition at different museums and galleries.

did.unittitle: Museum of Modern Art, Photographs by Robert Adams and Emmet Gowin
Museum of Modern Art, Three Americans
Andrew Roth, Eden and Commercial Residential
San Francisco Museum of Modern Art, Turning Back

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 17 posters for Robert Adams's photography exhibitions at galleries and museums in the United States and abroad.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and computer printouts with reviews of Robert Adams's exhibitions and books.

did.unittitle: Our Lives and Our Children reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early writings by Robert Adams, 1959-1972. Includes papers Adams wrote for his coursework at University of Redlands and University of Southern California and printed material, primarily journals containing Adams's academic writing. Also includes a typescript entitled "Summer 1968" which details a trip Adams took to Finland, Norway, Sweden, and Germany. Also present is a typescript entitled "With Wahoo," which contains an account of rafting trips Adams took with his father Ross Adams between 1953 and 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork created by Robert Adams, primarily pencil drawings and pen and wash drawings with a few prints. Most of the drawings depict nature, including landscapes and flowers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 5 years after the deaths of Robert Adams and Kerstin Adams. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert Adams's alphabetical files, which contain correspondence, notes, clippings, and other papers documenting his career and personal life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VII is organized into two subseries: Computer Media and Audiovisual Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Computer and audiovisual material documenting the career of Robert Adams. Includes recordings of interviews with Adams and photographs and videos of his exhibitions.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
